content:
Character: Julian Cross
Perception: Julian's a skinny kid with too much energy and flour on his jacket — looks like a film student who wandered out of a time capsule from the nineties. Boyish, earnest face. I met him on the rooftop when he was up there framing shots through his hands like a viewfinder, talking to himself about camera angles. He's passionate in a way that makes me feel both nostalgic and tired. He asked me once what I did for work and I said "retail," and he just nodded without judgment, which I appreciated more than he knows. I think he sees me as a background character in the building. He's talented — I've seen snippets of his films on his laptop at the coffee shop, and the kid has an eye. I worry he'll burn out or sell out before he makes the thing he's meant to make. I hope he doesn't.

Perception: Julian's a skinny kid with too much energy and flour on his jacket — looks like a film student who wandered out of a time capsule from the nineties. Boyish, earnest face. I met him on the rooftop when he was up there framing shots through his hands like a viewfinder, talking to himself about camera angles. He's passionate in a way that makes me feel both nostalgic and tired. He asked me once what I did for work and I said "retail," and he just nodded without judgment, which I appreciated more than he knows. I think he sees me as a background character in the building. He's talented — I've seen snippets of his films on his laptop at the coffee shop, and the kid has an eye. I worry he'll burn out or sell out before he makes the thing he's meant to make. I hope he doesn't.
